name: ECDH Shared Secret (X25519)
description: X25519 ECDH key exchange test vectors. MeshCore uses Ed25519 keys transposed to X25519 for key exchange. The resulting 32-byte shared secret is used as: AES key (bytes 0-15) and HMAC key (bytes 0-31).

vectors.description: RFC 7748 Test Vector: X25519 key exchange (Alice's perspective)
vectors.notes: RFC 7748 Section 6.1. Alice's private key and Bob's public key produce the shared secret. Note: MeshCore transposes Ed25519 keys to X25519, so actual ECDH inputs differ from raw Ed25519 keys.

vectors.description: RFC 7748 Test Vector: X25519 key exchange (Bob's perspective)
vectors.notes: RFC 7748 Section 6.1. Bob's private key and Alice's public key produce the same shared secret as ecdh-001, verifying the commutative property of ECDH.

vectors.description: X25519 with all-zeros private key scalar (edge case)
vectors.notes: Edge case: all-zero private key after clamping becomes a low-order scalar. X25519 with a clamped all-zero key (which becomes 0x40...00) against any public key. The all-zero shared secret indicates this is a degenerate key and implementations SHOULD reject it.
